output_summary: aligned schemas for datasets: discogs, lastfm, musicbrainz; generated 2 schema correspondence groups.
profiled datasets: discogs, lastfm, musicbrainz.
Reused the blocking configuration from state.
Blocking uses `semantic_similarity` for discogs_lastfm, musicbrainz_lastfm, blocking on name, artist; `sorted_neighbourhood` for discogs_musicbrainz, blocking on name.
Performance-wise, pair completeness ranges from 0.90 to 0.96 and candidate counts range from 47615 to 225831.
The config also records dataset id columns.

matcher mode `ml` with config source reused from state; evaluated pairs: id_columns, matching_strategies; selected scores: matching_strategies (threshold=0.7, f1=0.9549549549549549, threshold=0.75, f1=0.9473684210526316, f1=0.9605263157894737); produced 2 pair strategy entries.
This run was an initial draft of the pipeline.
The pipeline was generated to use upstream blocking and matching outputs.
The fusion strategy was defined with 'longest_string' fuser for seven attributes: name, artist, label, genre, release-country, release-date, and duration.
The fusion strategy also uses 'union' fuser for three attributes related to tracks: tracks_track_name, tracks_track_position, and tracks_track_duration.
Singleton retention was disabled in the fusion behavior.
The fusion behavior focuses on using the 'longest_string' method as the dominant fuser for most attributes, with 'union' used for track-related attributes.
The pipeline does not include singletons in the fusion output.
The pipeline supports CSV loading.
No changes were made to thresholds or alignment fields.
The fusion strategy covers a total of 10 fields with 7 using 'longest_string' and 3 using 'union'.
Using 'longest_string' as the dominant fuser ensures that the most complete string value is retained for key attributes, which is suitable for music metadata.
Applying 'union' for track-related attributes allows combining multiple track details from different sources, preserving all unique track information.
pipeline execution succeeded on attempt 1; fusion_data.csv was produced.

Added extensive new code in pipeline.py for music dataset entity matching and fusion: loaded discogs, lastfm, musicbrainz CSVs; defined blocking with EmbeddingBlocker and SortedNeighbourhoodBlocker; set up string, numeric, date comparators with FeatureExtractor; loaded ground truth pairs; extracted training features; performed model selection via GridSearchCV over RandomForest, LogisticRegression, GradientBoosting, SVM; matched entities with MLBasedMatcher.
This run was an initial draft of the evaluation adaption.
The dominant evaluation strategy used was a custom DataFusionStrategy named 'music_fusion_strategy' with tailored evaluation functions for specific columns.

Core attributes such as 'name', 'artist', 'duration', 'release-date', 'release-country', 'label', and 'tracks_track_name' were assigned specific evaluation functions like tokenized_match, numeric_tolerance_match, year_only_match, and set_equality_match.
Other detected list-like columns were evaluated using set_equality_match.
Added logic to build an evaluation-aligned view of fused data by mapping fused records to gold standard IDs using source IDs and detected prefixes.
Implemented detection and normalization of list-like columns across fused and gold datasets.
Included ID alignment diagnostics to compare coverage between fused IDs and gold IDs.
Added XML loading support for the gold standard dataset.
Configured evaluation functions registration and evaluation execution with debug output.
Persisted evaluation results to JSON files.
The evaluation adaption fits the current run as it aligns fused data with gold standard IDs to enable accurate evaluation.
evaluation execution succeeded on attempt 1; pipeline_evaluation.json is available.
overall accuracy is 53.81%; evaluation attempt counter is 1.
The main problem in this run is that the data fusion step produces false positives at the attribute level due to over-aggregation and incorrect conflict resolution.

Specifically, fusion merges multiple editions of the same album into one cluster, causing track lists to be supersets of the gold standard and picking wrong representative values for attributes like name, duration, and release-country.
This results in low accuracy for complex list-like attributes such as tracks_track_name (≈0.09) and tracks_track_duration (≈0.10), and moderate to low accuracy for duration (≈0.39) and name (≈0.48).
Overall accuracy is approximately 0.54 with macro accuracy around 0.53.
High accuracy is observed for simple scalar attributes like release-date (0.96), release-country (0.87), and artist (0.70).
Very low accuracy is observed for complex list-like attributes: tracks_track_name (0.09), tracks_track_duration (0.10), duration (0.39), and name (0.48).
Detailed mismatches show that fused track lists are supersets of expected lists, including duplicates and extra tracks from different editions.
Fused names often correspond to particular editions or variants rather than the canonical title.
This run is an evaluation refinement of the existing pipeline.
No changes were made to attribute fusers or thresholds in this run; the fusion strategy remains using 'longest_string' for most attributes and 'union' for track-related list attributes.
Singleton retention remains disabled.
CSV loading support was introduced.
There was no shift in fusion or matching behavior; the fusion strategy continues to use 'longest_string' for seven attributes and 'union' for three track-related attributes.
The pipeline continues to use pre-computed blocking and matching configurations with high pair completeness and F1 scores.
Cluster analysis confirms clusters are small and healthy with no one-to-many or ambiguous matches.
No semantic normalization or edition-aware fusion was introduced in this run.
The evaluation revealed that dominant errors arise from fusion-level false positives due to over-aggregation and naive conflict resolution using 'longest_string' and 'union'.
Blocking and matching are already well-tuned and not the main source of errors, so the focus remains on fusion logic.
pipeline execution succeeded on attempt 1; fusion_data.csv was produced.

pipeline.py updated comments for dataset loading, id column ensuring, attribute descriptions; evaluation.py added imports longest_string and union from PyDI.fusion; modified list_normalization import logic to check multiple candidate paths; enhanced build_eval_view to parse _fusion_sources into list of source ids, detect gold id prefix, map fused records to gold ids with eval_id and _eval_cluster_id columns; added list-like column detection and normalization with exclude_columns set expanded.
This run was an evaluation refinement.

The dominant evaluation strategy was to use tailored evaluation functions per attribute, including tokenized_match for textual fields, numeric_tolerance_match for numeric fields, year_only_match for dates, and set_equality_match for list-like attributes.
Simple scalar fields such as release-date, release-country, and artist showed high correctness.
Complex list-like attributes such as tracks_track_name and tracks_track_duration showed very low accuracy.
Attributes like name and duration had moderate to low accuracy due to fusion errors.
The evaluation code was refined to improve ID alignment by mapping fused records to gold IDs using source IDs and detected prefixes.
List-like columns were detected and normalized before evaluation.
Evaluation functions were explicitly registered per attribute with appropriate matching functions.
The evaluation output was saved in JSON format with debug information.
Code cleanup included removing unused fusion functions like longest_string and union from the evaluation script.
The evaluation refinement was motivated by the identification that dominant errors were due to fusion-level false positives rather than blocking or matching errors.
evaluation execution succeeded on attempt 1; pipeline_evaluation.json is available.
overall accuracy is 53.81%; evaluation attempt counter is 2.
The dominant problem in this run is that the fusion logic is causing systematic attribute-level errors, especially for track list attributes and duration.
The fusion uses 'union' for track lists, which leads to over-inclusion of tracks from multiple sources, causing false negatives in evaluation due to strict set equality matching.
The fusion uses 'longest_string' for scalar textual attributes and duration, which is a poor heuristic that often selects incorrect or non-canonical values.
These fusion errors cause low accuracies for attributes like tracks_track_name (0.087), tracks_track_duration (0.20), duration (0.39), and name (0.435).
Blocking and matching components are healthy and not the main cause of errors.
Tracks_track_name accuracy is 0.087 and tracks_track_duration accuracy is 0.20, indicating severe errors in track list fusion.
Duration accuracy is 0.39, with fused durations often much smaller or off-range compared to gold values.
Name accuracy is 0.435 and label accuracy is 0.625, showing fusion selects wrong variants or labels.
Debug JSONL shows fused track lists are supersets of gold, e.g., fused durations have more elements than expected.
This run was an initial draft of the pipeline.
The pipeline added fusion functions for attributes: duration, tracks_track_duration, label, release-date, tracks_track_position, release-country, tracks_track_name, genre, artist, and name.
The fusion strategy uses 'longest_string' for seven scalar attributes and 'union' for three track list attributes (tracks_track_name, tracks_track_position, tracks_track_duration).
Singleton retention was disabled in this pipeline.
The fusion behavior relies primarily on 'longest_string' for scalar attributes, which tends to select the longest textual variant regardless of source reliability or majority consensus.
For track list attributes, the fusion uses 'union', which merges all elements from all sources, resulting in over-inclusive lists that are supersets of the gold standard track lists.
The pipeline uses upstream blocking and matching outputs with high pair completeness and healthy cluster characteristics, indicating no changes to blocking or matching strategies.
No changes were made to thresholds or alignment fields.
The pipeline supports CSV loading and disables singleton inclusion.
The initial draft focuses on fusion improvements since blocking and matching are already performing well.
pipeline execution succeeded on attempt 1; fusion_data.csv was produced.

Changed evaluation.py with added import os; revised docstrings for _parse_source_ids and _detect_gold_prefix for clarity; expanded build_eval_view docstring and logic comments; replaced deprecated variable fusion_test_set_norm with fusion_test_set after normalize_list_like_columns call; updated ID coverage diagnostics to handle missing columns safely; added detailed print statements for evaluation metrics and functions.
This run was an initial draft of the evaluation adaption.
The dominant evaluation strategy used was to identify dominant error types from debug events and metrics, focusing on attribute accuracies and mismatches.
The evaluation used tailored evaluation functions per attribute, including tokenized_match, numeric_tolerance_match, year_only_match, and set_equality_match.
The evaluation focused on fusion logic errors rather than blocking or matching errors.

Track-level attributes (tracks_track_name, tracks_track_duration, tracks_track_position) showed very low accuracies and dominant errors due to fused lists being supersets of gold lists, causing false negatives under set equality evaluation.

Album-level duration attribute showed low accuracy with fused values often much smaller or off-range compared to gold, due to incorrect fusion logic treating duration as text and picking longest string.
Scalar textual attributes like label and name showed systematic mismatches due to fusion selecting longest string variants rather than canonical or majority values.
Attributes like release-date, release-country, and artist had relatively higher accuracies but still some mismatches.
evaluation execution succeeded on attempt 1; pipeline_evaluation.json is available.
overall accuracy is 47.21%; previous logged accuracy was 53.81%; evaluation attempt counter is 3.

Added extensive new code in pipeline.py implementing data loading for discogs, lastfm, musicbrainz datasets; defined blocking strategies using EmbeddingBlocker and SortedNeighbourhoodBlocker with specific columns and parameters; set up comparators and feature extractors for three dataset pairs with string, numeric, and date comparators; loaded ground truth pairs for training; extracted features for training.
